title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement s..j.lortz@gmail.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: stats2data_application
text: vignette walks two empirical examples based published studies whose raw data publicly available: ANOVA design reported cell means F-statistics (Reynolds & Besner, 2008); multiple linear regression reported descriptives, correlations, regression coefficients (Bardwell et al., 2007). Across two examples, every exported function stats2data demonstrated: optimisation functions optim_aov(), optim_vec(), optim_mlr(), parallel_optim(); S3 inspection methods print(), summary(), get_stats(), get_rmse(), coef(); diagnostic comparison plots plot_error(), plot_cooling(), plot_summary(), plot() (parallel results), plot_histogram(), plot_partial_regression(); GRIM helper check_grim(); Shiny entry point run_app(). Tuning hyperparameters left package defaults wherever feasible.

previous_headings: Overview
title: stats2data_application
code: if (!requireNamespace("devtools", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("sebastian-lortz/stats2data")

previous_headings: Overview
title: stats2data_application
code: library(stats2data) library(gridExtra) set.seed(2026)

title: stats2data_application
text: context Open Science Collaboration’s replication effort (Open Science Collaboration, 2015), original data Reynolds Besner (2008, https://osf.io/hasfu/) available. authors reported 2 × 2 within-subjects design measuring response times exception words nonwords predictable switch stay sequences. reported summary statistics encoded targets optim_aov().

previous_headings: 1. ANOVA module — Reynolds & Besner (2008)
title: stats2data_application
text: Factor2 Factor1:Factor2 interaction reported F < 1; placeholder targets supplied : plausible response-time range derived reported pooled MSE = 3070:
code: S                  <- 16 levels             <- c(2, 2) target_group_means <- c(543, 536, 614, 618) factor_type        <- c("within", "within") formula            <- "outcome ~ Factor1 * Factor2 + Error(ID / (Factor1 * Factor2))" integer            <- FALSE target_f_list <- list(   effect = c("Factor1", "Factor2", "Factor1:Factor2"),   F_value      = c(30.5, .5, .5) ) L <- min(target_group_means) - floor(4 * sqrt(3070)) U <- max(target_group_means) + ceiling(5 * sqrt(3070)) c(L = L, U = U) #>   L   U  #> 315 896 range <- c(315, 896)

previous_headings: 1. ANOVA module — Reynolds & Besner (2008)
title: stats2data_application
text: optim_aov() called default arguments (max_iter = 1e4, thresh = 5e-3, init_temp = 1e-3, cooling_rate = NULL (set automatically), max_starts = 3).
code: result.aov <- optim_aov(   S                  = S,   levels             = levels,   target_group_means = target_group_means,   target_f_list      = target_f_list,   factor_type        = factor_type,   range              = range,   formula            = formula,   integer            = integer   )

previous_headings: 1. ANOVA module — Reynolds & Besner (2008)
title: stats2data_application
text: default print method shows design, sample size, target effects, best objective value: summary() prints target-vs-simulated F-values, target-vs-simulated cell means, per-block RMSE: get_stats() returns underlying numeric components: fitted afex ANOVA table, F-vector, cell means computed simulated data. get_rmse() returns F-statistic group-mean RMSE separately:
code: result.aov #> stats2data ANOVA result #>   Design:    2 x 2 (within, within) #>   Subjects:   16  #>   Effects:    Factor1, Factor2, Factor1:Factor2  #>   Best error: 0.00079433 summary(result.aov) #> stats2data ANOVA Summary #> ----------------------------------------------- #> Design:     2 x 2 (within, within) #> Subjects:   16  #> Best error: 0.00079433  #>  #> RMSE #>   F-statistics: 0.0007943  #>   Group means:  9.392e-13  #>  #> ----------------------------------------------- #> F-values (Target vs. Simulated): #>           effect target_F   sim_F #>          Factor1     30.5 30.4996 #>          Factor2      0.5  0.5013 #>  Factor1:Factor2      0.5  0.4997 #>  #> ----------------------------------------------- #> Group Means (Target vs. Simulated): #>        cell target_mean sim_mean #>  F1=1, F2=1         543      543 #>  F1=1, F2=2         536      536 #>  F1=2, F2=1         614      614 #>  F1=2, F2=2         618      618 stats.aov <- get_stats(result.aov) stats.aov #> $model #> Anova Table (Type 3 tests) #>  #> Response: outcome #>                 num Df den Df     MSE       F      ges    Pr(>F)     #> Factor1              1     15 3070.07 30.4996 0.156900 5.852e-05 *** #> Factor2              1     15   71.82  0.5013 0.000072    0.4898     #> Factor1:Factor2      1     15  968.59  0.4997 0.000961    0.4905     #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> $F_value #> [1] 30.4995871  0.5012765  0.4996953 #>  #> $mean #> 1_1 1_2 2_1 2_2  #> 543 536 614 618 get_rmse(result.aov) #> $rmse_F #> [1] 0.0007943298 #>  #> $rmse_mean #> [1] 9.392074e-13

previous_headings: 1. ANOVA module — Reynolds & Besner (2008)
title: stats2data_application
text: plot_error() shows simulated-annealing best-error trajectory:  plot_cooling() reconstructs temperature schedule stored inputs:
code: plot_error(result.aov) plot_cooling(result.aov)

previous_headings: 1. ANOVA module — Reynolds & Besner (2008)
title: stats2data_application
text: plot_summary() builds lollipop chart simulated minus target values. standardised = FALSE (used one target F 0), y-axis original F-scale; standardised = TRUE (package default) discrepancy divided target value, fallback unstandardised difference near-zero targets.
code: plot_summary(result.aov, standardised = FALSE)

previous_headings: 1. ANOVA module — Reynolds & Besner (2008)
title: stats2data_application
text: simulated dataset stored result.aov$data columns ID, Factor1, Factor2, outcome: can vizualise outcomes within groups density plot:
code: data.aov <- result.aov$data head(data.aov) #>   ID Factor1 Factor2  outcome #> 1  1       1       1 530.8676 #> 2  1       1       2 450.7417 #> 3  1       2       1 478.9247 #> 4  1       2       2 553.8410 #> 5  2       1       1 562.7350 #> 6  2       1       2 557.6526 library(ggplot2)  ggplot(data.aov,        aes(x = outcome,            fill = interaction(Factor1, Factor2, sep = " \u00d7 "))) +   geom_density(alpha = 0.3, color = "gray20") +   scale_fill_grey(start = 0.85, end = 0.25,                   name = "Factor1 \u00d7 Factor2") +   labs(x = "Outcome", y = "Density") +   xlim(200,1000) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

what: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007)
title: stats2data_application
text: Bardwell et al. (2007, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jad.2006.06.013) examined whether mood symptoms apnea severity better predict daytime fatigue patients obstructive sleep apnea. original raw data Bardwell et al. (2003) publicly available. reconstruction proceeds three steps: marginal distributions via optim_vec(), joint regression structure via optim_mlr(), replication assessment via parallel_optim().

previous_headings: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007) > 2.1 Step 1 — marginals via optim_vec()
title: stats2data_application
code: N           <- 60 target_mean <- c(48.8, 17.3, 12.6, 10.8) names(target_mean) <- c("Apnea.1", "Apnea.2", "Depression", "Fatigue") target_sd   <- c(27.1, 20.1, 11.3, 7.3) integer     <- c(FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE) range_matrix <- matrix(   c( 15,   0,  0,  0,     111, 80.9, 49, 28),   nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE )

previous_headings: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007) > 2.1 Step 1 — marginals via optim_vec()
title: stats2data_application
text: GRIM test (Brown & Heathers, 2017) verifies whether reported mean achievable stated sample size decimal precision. integer-valued mean checked one decimal place:
code: check_grim(n = N, target_mean = target_mean["Depression"], decimals = 1) #> $test #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $grim_mean #> Depression  #>       12.6 check_grim(n = N, target_mean = target_mean["Fatigue"],    decimals = 1) #> $test #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $grim_mean #> Fatigue  #>    10.8

previous_headings: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007) > 2.1 Step 1 — marginals via optim_vec()
title: stats2data_application
text: set sprite_prec = c(1, 1),thresh = .005, set reported rounding precision. , run optimization default arguments use defaults (thresh = .005, max_iter = 5e5, init_temp = cooling_rate = NULL (set automatically), max_starts = 3).
code: result.vec <- optim_vec(   N           = N,   target_mean = target_mean,   target_sd   = target_sd,   range       = range_matrix,   integer     = integer,   sprite_prec = c(1, 1),   thresh = .05 )

previous_headings: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007) > 2.1 Step 1 — marginals via optim_vec()
title: stats2data_application
code: result.vec #> stats2data Descriptives result #>   Variables: 4  #>   N:         60  #>   Best error per variable: #>     Apnea.1: 0.04396 #>     Apnea.2: 0.04991 #>     Depression: 0.04657 #>     Fatigue: 0.04637 summary(result.vec) #> stats2data Descriptives Summary #> ----------------------------------------------- #> N: 60  | Variables: 4  #>  #> Best error per variable: #>   Apnea.1: 0.04396 #>   Apnea.2: 0.04991 #>   Depression: 0.04657 #>   Fatigue: 0.04637 #>  #> RMSE #>   Means: 0.03991  #>   SDs:   0.04675  #>  #> Target vs. Simulated: #>    variable target_mean sim_mean target_sd sim_sd #>     Apnea.1        48.8    48.75      27.1 27.056 #>     Apnea.2        17.3    17.35      20.1 20.050 #>  Depression        12.6    12.63      11.3 11.347 #>     Fatigue        10.8    10.78       7.3  7.346 get_stats(result.vec) #> $mean #>    Apnea.1    Apnea.2 Depression    Fatigue  #>   48.75005   17.34988   12.63333   10.78333  #>  #> $sd #>    Apnea.1    Apnea.2 Depression    Fatigue  #>  27.056041  20.050089  11.346569   7.346374 get_rmse(result.vec) #> $rmse_mean #> [1] 0.03991102 #>  #> $rmse_sd #> [1] 0.04675118

previous_headings: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007) > 2.1 Step 1 — marginals via optim_vec()
title: stats2data_application
text: plot_error() stats2data_vec object accepts run select per-variable trajectory; trajectory Fatigue (fourth variable) shown:
code: plot_error(result.vec, run = 2) plot_cooling(result.vec)

previous_headings: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007) > 2.1 Step 1 — marginals via optim_vec()
title: stats2data_application
code: plot_summary(result.vec, standardised = FALSE)

previous_headings: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007) > 2.1 Step 1 — marginals via optim_vec()
title: stats2data_application
code: data.vec <- result.vec$data head(data.vec) #>     Apnea.1     Apnea.2 Depression Fatigue #> 1  45.71396  1.91228940          4      12 #> 2  57.34349 34.83918618         29       7 #> 3 105.43328  0.07687778          8       4 #> 4  64.91423 37.56782867         30      19 #> 5  67.00977  1.81325274         23       2 #> 6  29.96682  2.28155683          4      14  library(ggplot2)  cont_vars <- c("Apnea.1", "Apnea.2") int_vars  <- c("Depression", "Fatigue")  ggdata.vec <- tidyr::pivot_longer(   data.vec,   cols      = c(Apnea.1, Apnea.2, Depression, Fatigue),   names_to  = "variable",   values_to = "value" ) ggdata.vec$variable <- factor(   ggdata.vec$variable,   levels = c(cont_vars, int_vars) ) ggdata.vec$kind <- ifelse(ggdata.vec$variable %in% int_vars,                           "integer", "continuous")  ggplot(ggdata.vec, aes(x = value)) +   geom_density(     data  = subset(ggdata.vec, kind == "continuous"),     fill  = "gray60", color = "gray20", alpha = 0.5   ) +   geom_histogram(     data     = subset(ggdata.vec, kind == "integer"),     aes(y    = after_stat(density)),     binwidth = 1,     fill     = "gray60", color = "gray20"   ) +   facet_wrap(~ variable, scales = "free", nrow = 2) +   labs(x = NULL, y = "Density") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

previous_headings: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007) > 2.2 Step 2 — regression structure via optim_mlr()
title: stats2data_application
text: reported regression coefficients (intercept first), standard errors, upper-triangular correlation matrix (column-wise, excluding diagonal) encoded numeric vectors. NA entries flag unreported values excluded objective.
code: target_reg <- c(4.020, 0.023, 0.008, 0.438) names(target_reg) <- c("(Intercept)", "Apnea.1", "Apnea.2", "Depression") target_se   <- c(NA, 0.034, 0.048, 0.066) target_cor  <- c(NA, NA, NA, 0.11, 0.20, 0.68) reg_equation <- "Fatigue ~ Apnea.1 + Apnea.2 + Depression"

previous_headings: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007) > 2.2 Step 2 — regression structure via optim_mlr()
title: stats2data_application
text: compile-time reasons local-refinement step disabled (hill_climbs = NULL; default 1e4), max_starts reduced 1 (default 3), max_iter reduced 1e4 (default 1e5). arguments use defaults (weight = 0.5, thresh = 5e-3, init_temp = NULL adaptive initialisation, cooling_rate = NULL).
code: result.mlr <- optim_mlr(   N            = N,   target_mean  = target_mean,   target_sd    = target_sd,   range        = range_matrix,   integer      = integer,   reg_equation = reg_equation,   target_cor   = target_cor,   target_reg   = target_reg,   target_se    = target_se,   thresh       = .0005,   weight = .05, ) #>  #> Hill climbing best error: 0.0004525559

previous_headings: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007) > 2.2 Step 2 — regression structure via optim_mlr()
title: stats2data_application
text: get_stats() returns fitted lm, coefficients standard errors, upper-triangular correlation vector, column-wise means SDs: coef() convenience wrapper re-fits regression returns coefficient vector:
code: result.mlr #> stats2data MLR result #>   N:          60  #>   Equation:   Fatigue ~ Apnea.1 + Apnea.2 + Depression  #>   Best error: 0.000452556 summary(result.mlr) #> stats2data MLR Summary #> ----------------------------------------------- #> N: 60  | Model: Fatigue ~ Apnea.1 + Apnea.2 + Depression  #> Best error: 0.000452556  #>  #> RMSE #>   Correlations:             0.00139  #>   Regression Coefficients:  0.0004172  #>   Standard Errors:          0.001254  #>  #> Descriptives (Target vs. Simulated): #>    variable target_mean sim_mean target_sd sim_sd #>     Apnea.1        48.8     48.8      27.1 27.100 #>     Apnea.2        17.3     17.3      20.1 20.100 #>  Depression        12.6     12.6      11.3 11.296 #>     Fatigue        10.8     10.8       7.3  7.302 #>  #> Coefficients (Target vs. Simulated): #>         term target_reg  sim_reg target_se  sim_se #>  (Intercept)      4.020 4.020067        NA 1.70406 #>      Apnea.1      0.023 0.023339     0.034 0.03403 #>      Apnea.2      0.008 0.007357     0.048 0.04668 #>   Depression      0.438 0.437596     0.066 0.06428 #>  #> Correlations (Target vs. Simulated): #>                pair target_cor sim_cor #>     Apnea.1-Apnea.2         NA 0.63345 #>  Apnea.1-Depression         NA 0.01334 #>     Apnea.1-Fatigue         NA 0.18448 #>  Apnea.2-Depression       0.11 0.10848 #>     Apnea.2-Fatigue       0.20 0.20001 #>  Depression-Fatigue       0.68 0.68187 stats.mlr <- get_stats(result.mlr) stats.mlr$reg #> (Intercept)     Apnea.1     Apnea.2  Depression  #> 4.020067413 0.023338825 0.007357097 0.437595632 stats.mlr$se #> (Intercept)     Apnea.1     Apnea.2  Depression  #>  1.70405852  0.03403070  0.04667936  0.06427530 stats.mlr$cor #> [1] 0.63345280 0.01334132 0.18448294 0.10847972 0.20001157 0.68186668 coef(result.mlr) #> (Intercept)     Apnea.1     Apnea.2  Depression  #> 4.020067413 0.023338825 0.007357097 0.437595632 get_rmse(result.mlr) #> $rmse_cor #> [1] 0.001389951 #>  #> $rmse_reg #> [1] 0.0004171884 #>  #> $rmse_se #> [1] 0.001254274

previous_headings: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007) > 2.2 Step 2 — regression structure via optim_mlr()
title: stats2data_application
text: plot_error() shows total-objective trajectory:  stats2data_mlr objects, plot_error() exposes ratio = TRUE switch plots per-iteration correlation--regression error ratio together mean, median, final reference lines:  observe regression errors dominate objective function, thus, assign priori weight regression component compared correlation component weight = .05. run optimization , plot errror ratio:  see balanced contribution components, lowered objective value, increasing match target summary statistics.
code: plot_error(result.mlr, first_iter = 1000) plot_error(result.mlr, ratio = TRUE) result.mlr <- optim_mlr(   N            = N,   target_mean  = target_mean,   target_sd    = target_sd,   range        = range_matrix,   integer      = integer,   reg_equation = reg_equation,   target_cor   = target_cor,   target_reg   = target_reg,   target_se    = target_se,   thresh       = .0005,   weight = .05, ) #>  #> Hill climbing best error: 0.0007000829 plot_error(result.mlr, ratio = TRUE)

previous_headings: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007) > 2.2 Step 2 — regression structure via optim_mlr()
title: stats2data_application
code: plot_summary(result.mlr, standardised = FALSE)

previous_headings: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007) > 2.2 Step 2 — regression structure via optim_mlr()
title: stats2data_application
text: plot_partial_regression() returns one partial-regression ggplot per predictor, annotated partial slope residual SD:
code: data.mlr <- result.mlr$data head(data.mlr) #>     Apnea.1   Apnea.2 Depression Fatigue #> 1 102.13561 80.899985          5      11 #> 2 111.00000 15.409149          0      11 #> 3  17.48620 13.285775         26      22 #> 4  43.61846 14.551106         25       9 #> 5  48.71794  4.559079          2       4 #> 6 110.99886  9.806297         12      21 partial.plots <- plot_partial_regression(lm(reg_equation, data = data.mlr)) gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = partial.plots, ncol = 2)

previous_headings: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007)
title: stats2data_application
text: parallel_optim() repeatedly calls optim_* function arguments, returns list results, identifies single best run minimum objective error. runs = 18 parallel replications used compile-time reasons; setting cores = 6 dispatches runs multiple R sessions via future::multisession.
code: result.parallel <- parallel_optim(   FUN = optim_mlr,   args = list(     N            = N,     target_mean  = target_mean,     target_sd    = target_sd,     range        = range_matrix,     integer      = integer,     reg_equation = reg_equation,     target_cor   = target_cor,     target_reg   = target_reg,     target_se    = target_se,     thresh       = .0005,     weight       = .05   ),   runs  = 18,   cores = 6,   seed  = 2026 )

previous_headings: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007) > 2.3 Step 3 — repeated runs via parallel_optim()
title: stats2data_application
text: get_stats() stats2data_parallel object aggregates numeric component returned per-run get_stats() method data frame columns mean, median, sd, min, max: get_rmse() returns three components: between_rmse (per-metric run--run RMSE across-run grand mean), target_rmse (per-metric RMSE original targets), raw per-run vectors:

previous_headings: 2. Descriptives + MLR module — Bardwell et al. (2007) > 2.3 Step 3 — repeated runs via parallel_optim()
title: stats2data_application
text: plot() method stats2data_parallel displays -run RMSE distribution alongside deviation--target RMSE distribution, faceted metric:  single best run accessible via $best supports S3 methods stand-alone result:
code: plot_summary(result.parallel) result.parallel$best #> stats2data MLR result #>   N:          60  #>   Equation:   Fatigue ~ Apnea.1 + Apnea.2 + Depression  #>   Best error: 0.000415224 get_rmse(result.parallel$best) #> $rmse_cor #> [1] 0.001968488 #>  #> $rmse_reg #> [1] 0.0003886003 #>  #> $rmse_se #> [1] 0.001088147

title: stats2data_application
text: point--click interface three modules launched via:
code: run_app()

title: stats2data_application
text: Bardwell, W. ., Moore, P., Ancoli-Israel, S., & Dimsdale, J. E. (2003). Fatigue obstructive sleep apnea: driven depressive symptoms instead apnea severity?. American journal psychiatry, 160(2), 350–355. https://doi.org/10.1176/appi.ajp.160.2.350 Bardwell, W. ., Ancoli-Israel, S., & Dimsdale, J. E. (2007). Comparison effects depressive symptoms apnea severity fatigue patients obstructive sleep apnea: replication study. Journal affective disorders, 97(1-3), 181–186. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jad.2006.06.013 Brown, N. J. L., & Heathers, J. . J. (2017). GRIM Test: Simple Technique Detects Numerous Anomalies Reporting Results Psychology. Social Psychological Personality Science, 8 (4), 363–369. https://doi.org/10.1177/1948550616673876 Open Science Collaboration. (2015). Estimating reproducibility psychological science. Science, 349 (6251), aac4716. https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aac4716 Reynolds, M., & Besner, D. (2008). Contextual effects reading aloud: Evidence pathway control. Journal Experimental Psychology: Learning, Memory, Cognition, 34(1), 50–64. https://doi.org/10.1037/0278-7393.34.1.50

previous_headings: Usage
title: Data Simulation from Summary Statistics
text: can use app https://sebastian-lortz.shinyapps.io/stats2data/. Expect longer computation time compared running app locally.

previous_headings: Usage
title: Data Simulation from Summary Statistics
text: stats2data package build R Version 4.4.2 using Apple clang version 16.0.0 (clang-1600.0.26.6) GNU Fortran (GCC) 14.2.0. compile R source, install appropriate toolchain - macOS: see https://mac.r-project.org/tools/ - windows: see https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/Rtools/

previous_headings: Usage
title: Data Simulation from Summary Statistics
text: can install latest version R package stats2data like :
code: # install devtools if needed if (!requireNamespace("devtools")) {install.packages("devtools")}  # install from GitHub devtools::install_github("sebastian-lortz/stats2data")

previous_headings: Usage
title: Data Simulation from Summary Statistics
text: can launch ShinyApp locally running:
code: stats2data::run_app()

title: Data Simulation from Summary Statistics
text: Please cite stats2data use . cite software, use: Lortz SAJ (2025). stats2data: Data Simulation Summary Statistics. R package version 0.0.1.000, https://sebastian-lortz.github.io/stats2data/, https://github.com/sebastian-lortz/stats2data. copy reference information BibTeX file:
code: @Manual{stats2data,   title        = {stats2data: Non-Parametric Data Simulation from Summary Statistics},   author       = {S. A. J. Lortz},   year         = {2026},   note         = {R package version 0.0.1.000},   url          = {https://github.com/sebastian-lortz/stats2data} }

title: Data Simulation from Summary Statistics
text: open feedback new ideas. Please mind Contributor Code Conduct.

title: Data Simulation from Summary Statistics
text: reading doc version: 0.0.1.000 README compiled 2026-05-03 22:06:10.

what: Check plausibility of a reported mean with the GRIM test — check_grim
title: Check plausibility of a reported mean with the GRIM test — check_grim
text: Performs GRIM (Granularity-Related Inconsistency Means) test (Brown & Heathers, 2017) assess whether reported mean numerically possible given sample size number decimal places.

title: Check plausibility of a reported mean with the GRIM test — check_grim
code: check_grim(n, target_mean, decimals, tol = .Machine$double.eps^0.5)

title: Check plausibility of a reported mean with the GRIM test — check_grim
text: n Integer. Sample size; must single positive whole number. target_mean Numeric. Reported mean tested plausibility. decimals Integer. Number decimal places reported mean. tol Numeric. thresh rounding errors; single non-negative value. Default .Machine$double.eps^0.5.

title: Check plausibility of a reported mean with the GRIM test — check_grim
text: list components: test Logical. TRUE reported mean plausible. grim_mean Numeric. test fails, nearest plausible mean     (rounded decimals); otherwise original     target_mean.

title: Check plausibility of a reported mean with the GRIM test — check_grim
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ check_grim(n = 10, target_mean = 3.7, decimals = 1) check_grim(n = 10, target_mean = 3.74, decimals = 2) } # }

what: Extract regression coefficients from a stats2data MLR result — coef.stats2data_mlr
title: Extract regression coefficients from a stats2data MLR result — coef.stats2data_mlr
text: Fits regression model stored object$inputs$reg_equation simulated data returns estimated coefficients.

title: Extract regression coefficients from a stats2data MLR result — coef.stats2data_mlr
code: # S3 method for class 'stats2data_mlr' coef(object, ...)

title: Extract regression coefficients from a stats2data MLR result — coef.stats2data_mlr
text: object object class stats2data_mlr. ... Additional arguments (unused).

title: Extract regression coefficients from a stats2data MLR result — coef.stats2data_mlr
text: named numeric vector regression coefficients.

what: Compute RMSE for a stats2data result — get_rmse.stats2data_aov
title: Compute RMSE for a stats2data result — get_rmse.stats2data_aov
text: Calculates root-mean-square error metrics comparing achieved summary statistics simulated data target inputs.

title: Compute RMSE for a stats2data result — get_rmse.stats2data_aov
code: # S3 method for class 'stats2data_aov' get_rmse(result, ...)  # S3 method for class 'stats2data_mlr' get_rmse(result, ...)  # S3 method for class 'stats2data_parallel' get_rmse(result, ...)  # S3 method for class 'stats2data_vec' get_rmse(result, ...)  get_rmse(result, ...)

title: Compute RMSE for a stats2data result — get_rmse.stats2data_aov
text: result stats2data result object (stats2data_vec, stats2data_mlr, stats2data_aov). ... Additional arguments passed methods.

title: Compute RMSE for a stats2data result — get_rmse.stats2data_aov
text: named list RMSE values. Contents depend class   result: stats2data_vec: Elements rmse_mean       rmse_sd. stats2data_mlr: Elements rmse_cor,       rmse_reg, rmse_se. stats2data_aov: Elements rmse_F       rmse_mean.

title: Compute RMSE for a stats2data result — get_rmse.stats2data_aov
text: stats2data_parallel objects, get_rmse returns list three elements: between_rmse Data frame.     Per-metric summary (Mean, SD, Min, Max) run--run RMSE, computed     grand mean across runs. target_rmse Data frame.     Per-metric summary RMSE run original targets. raw List numeric vectors target     holding per-run raw RMSE values, keyed metric name.

title: Compute RMSE for a stats2data result — get_rmse.stats2data_aov
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- optim_vec(   N = 50, target_mean = c(x = 5), target_sd = c(x = 1),   range = c(0, 10), integer = FALSE, sprite_prec = c(2, 2),   max_iter = 1e4, max_starts = 1, progress_mode = "off" ) get_rmse(res) } # }

what: Extract statistics from a `stats2data` result — get_stats.stats2data_aov
title: Extract statistics from a `stats2data` result — get_stats.stats2data_aov
text: Computes returns key analytical outputs `stats2data` result object, including means, standard deviations, correlations, regression coefficients, F-statistics depending module produced result.

title: Extract statistics from a `stats2data` result — get_stats.stats2data_aov
code: # S3 method for class 'stats2data_aov' get_stats(result, ...)  # S3 method for class 'stats2data_mlr' get_stats(result, ...)  # S3 method for class 'stats2data_parallel' get_stats(result, ...)  # S3 method for class 'stats2data_vec' get_stats(result, ...)  get_stats(result, ...)

title: Extract statistics from a `stats2data` result — get_stats.stats2data_aov
text: result stats2data result object (stats2data_vec, stats2data_mlr, stats2data_aov). ... Additional arguments passed methods.

title: Extract statistics from a `stats2data` result — get_stats.stats2data_aov
text: named list statistics. Contents depend class   result: stats2data_vec: Elements mean sd. stats2data_mlr: Elements model, reg,       se, cor, mean, sd. stats2data_aov: Elements model, F_value,       mean.

title: Extract statistics from a `stats2data` result — get_stats.stats2data_aov
text: stats2data_parallel objects, get_stats aggregates numeric component returned per-run get_stats method across runs.  result named list data frames, one per component (e.g., F_value, mean, reg, cor, sd), containing columns mean, median, sd, min, max.

title: Extract statistics from a `stats2data` result — get_stats.stats2data_aov
text: vector $mean returned **sorted-key** cell order (Factor1 fastest, Factor2, Factor3, ...). matches order optimiser uses internally order target_group_means consumed optim_aov. returned vector named cell identifier (e.g. "1_1", "1_2", "2_1", ...) cell value belongs unambiguous downstream.

title: Extract statistics from a `stats2data` result — get_stats.stats2data_aov
text: get_stats reuses formula stored result$inputs$formula rather reconstructing one factor names. guarantees model fitted one optimiser used, eliminating drift across afex versions design types (purely-within, purely-, mixed).

title: Extract statistics from a `stats2data` result — get_stats.stats2data_aov
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- optim_vec(   N = 50, target_mean = c(x = 5), target_sd = c(x = 1),   range = c(0, 10), integer = FALSE, sprite_prec = c(2, 2),   max_iter = 1e4, max_starts = 1, progress_mode = "off" ) get_stats(res) } # }

title: Perform hill-climbing optimization — hill_climb
text: Executes hill-climbing algorithm iteratively improve candidate data set minimizing supplied error function. Supports LM module.

title: Perform hill-climbing optimization — hill_climb
code: hill_climb(   current_candidate,   error_function,   N,   hill_climbs = 100,   num_preds = NULL,   neighborhood_size = 4,   progressor = NULL,   pb_interval = NULL,   progress_mode = "console" )

title: Perform hill-climbing optimization — hill_climb
text: current_candidate Matrix. predictor matrix optimized. error_function Function. Objective function takes candidate returns list element $total_error. N Integer. Number observations (rows) current_candidate. hill_climbs Integer. Maximum number iterations. Default 1e2. num_preds Integer. Number predictor columns. neighborhood_size Integer. Number candidate moves evaluated per iteration. Default 4. progressor Function NULL. progressr progressor callback. Default NULL. pb_interval Integer NULL. Interval (iterations) progressor calls. Default NULL. progress_mode Character: "console", "shiny", "". Default "console".

title: Perform hill-climbing optimization — hill_climb
text: list components: best_candidate optimized candidate matrix achieving lowest error. best_error Numeric. minimum objective function value found.

title: Perform hill-climbing optimization — hill_climb
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ hill_climb(   current_candidate = matrix(rnorm(200), 100, 2),   error_function = function(cand) list(total_error = sum(cand^2)),   N = 100,   hill_climbs = 100,   num_preds = 2 ) } # }

what: Reshape Data from Long to Wide Format — long_to_wide
title: Reshape Data from Long to Wide Format — long_to_wide
text: Converts data.frame long format (ID column, optional -subject grouping columns, time column, one measurement columns) wide format.  Column names result follow pattern <measure>_<time>.

title: Reshape Data from Long to Wide Format — long_to_wide
code: long_to_wide(data)

title: Reshape Data from Long to Wide Format — long_to_wide
text: data data.frame long format.  first column taken subject identifier (ID); next columns time treated ‐subject factors; time column must named "time"; remaining columns measurements.

title: Reshape Data from Long to Wide Format — long_to_wide
text: data.frame wide format, one row per subject (‐subject   factor combination) one column per measure–time combination.

what: Optimize simulated data to match ANOVA F-values — optim_aov
title: Optimize simulated data to match ANOVA F-values — optim_aov
text: Uses simulated annealing generate raw data reproduce target ANOVA F-statistics group means specified factorial design.

title: Optimize simulated data to match ANOVA F-values — optim_aov
code: optim_aov(   S,   levels,   target_group_means,   target_f_list,   integer,   range,   formula,   factor_type,   subgroup_sizes = NULL,   thresh = 0.005,   max_iter = 10000,   init_temp = 0.001,   cooling_rate = NULL,   max_starts = 3,   progress_mode = "console" )

title: Optimize simulated data to match ANOVA F-values — optim_aov
text: S Integer. Total number subjects. levels Integer vector. Number levels per factor. target_group_means Numeric vector length prod(levels). Target cell means expand.grid order. target_f_list List components effect (character) F (numeric) equal length. integer Logical. Generate integer-valued data? range Numeric vector length 2. Bounds individual observations. formula Formula character. ANOVA model formula. factor_type Character vector (""/"within") matching length levels. subgroup_sizes Optional numeric vector -group sizes (must sum N). thresh Numeric. Convergence threshold. Default 1e-2. max_iter Integer. Iterations per restart. Default 1e3. init_temp Numeric. Initial SA temperature. Default 1. cooling_rate Numeric (0,1) NULL (auto). Default NULL. max_starts Integer. Number restarts. Default 1. progress_mode Character: "console", "shiny", "". Default "console".

title: Optimize simulated data to match ANOVA F-values — optim_aov
text: stats2data.object list components best_error,   data, inputs, adjusted_targets,   track_error.

what: Optimize simulated data to match target correlations and regression estimates — optim_mlr
title: Optimize simulated data to match target correlations and regression estimates — optim_mlr
text: Simulates data resulting correlations regression coefficients match specified targets given regression model. Internally calls optim_vec first generate marginals matching target means standard deviations, optimizes predictor orderings via simulated annealing (hill climbing) match correlation regression targets.

title: Optimize simulated data to match target correlations and regression estimates — optim_mlr
code: optim_mlr(   N,   target_mean,   target_sd,   range,   integer,   target_cor,   target_reg,   reg_equation,   sprite_prec = c(2, 2),   target_se = NULL,   weight = 0.5,   thresh = 0.005,   max_iter = 1e+05,   init_temp = NULL,   cooling_rate = NULL,   max_starts = 3,   hill_climbs = 10000,   progress_mode = "console" )

title: Optimize simulated data to match target correlations and regression estimates — optim_mlr
text: N Integer. Total number observations. target_mean Named numeric vector. Desired means variable (names must match variables reg_equation). target_sd Named numeric vector. Desired standard deviations variable (length names target_mean). range Numeric vector length 2 numeric matrix. Allowed value range variables (vector), per-variable bounds two-row matrix columns matching target_mean. integer Logical logical vector. TRUE, generate integer-valued data; length 1 length target_mean. target_cor Numeric vector. Target upper-triangular (excluding diagonal) correlation values predictor outcome variables. target_reg Numeric vector. Target regression coefficients including intercept, matching terms reg_equation. reg_equation Character. Regression model formula (e.g., "Y ~ X1 + X2 + X1:X2"). sprite_prec Integer vector length 2. Decimal precision mean SD using SPRITE integer data. Default c(2, 2). target_se Numeric vector NULL. Target standard errors regression coefficients (length target_reg). Default NULL. weight Numeric vector length 2. Weights correlation vs. regression error objective function. Default c(1, 1). thresh Numeric. Convergence threshold. Default 1e-6. max_iter Integer. Iterations per restart. Default 1e5. init_temp Numeric. Initial SA temperature. Default 1. cooling_rate Numeric (0,1) NULL (auto). Default NULL. max_starts Integer. Number restarts. Default 1. hill_climbs Integer NULL. Number hill-climbing iterations optional local refinement; NULL, skips refinement. Default NULL. progress_mode Character: "console", "shiny", "". Default "console".

title: Optimize simulated data to match target correlations and regression estimates — optim_mlr
text: stats2data.object list components: best_error Numeric. Minimum objective error achieved. data Data frame optimized predictor outcome values. optim_vec stats2data.object returned internal     optim_vec call (marginal optimization results). inputs List input parameters reproducibility. track_error Numeric vector best error iteration. track_error_ratio Numeric vector error ratios (cor vs. reg)     per iteration.

title: Optimize simulated data to match target correlations and regression estimates — optim_mlr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- optim_mlr(   N            = 100,   target_mean  = c(X1 = 5, X2 = 3, Y = 10),   target_sd    = c(X1 = 1, X2 = 2, Y = 3),   range        = c(0, 20),   integer      = FALSE,   target_cor   = c(.23, .10, .45),   target_reg   = c(2.1, 1.2, -0.8),   reg_equation = "Y ~ X1 + X2",   max_iter     = 10000,   hill_climbs  = 50 ) } # }

what: Optimize a vector or matrix to match target means and SDs — optim_vec
title: Optimize a vector or matrix to match target means and SDs — optim_vec
text: Uses stats2data approach simulate one multiple vectors matches specified target means standard deviations given input parameters.

title: Optimize a vector or matrix to match target means and SDs — optim_vec
code: optim_vec(   N,   target_mean,   target_sd,   range,   integer,   thresh = 0.005,   sprite_prec = c(2, 2),   max_iter = 5e+05,   init_temp = 0.001,   cooling_rate = NULL,   max_starts = 3,   progress_mode = "console" )

title: Optimize a vector or matrix to match target means and SDs — optim_vec
text: N Integer. Number values vector. target_mean Named numeric vector. Desired means variable. target_sd Named numeric vector. Desired standard deviations variable. range Numeric vector length 2 numeric matrix. Allowed value range variables (vector), per-variable bounds two-row matrix matching target_mean. integer Logical logical vector. TRUE, optimize integer values; length 1 length target_mean. thresh Numeric. Convergence threshold. Default 1e-3. sprite_prec Integer vector length 2. Decimal precision mean SD using SPRITE integer data. Default c(2, 2). max_iter Integer. Iterations per restart. Default 1e4. init_temp Numeric. Initial SA temperature. Default 1. cooling_rate Numeric (0,1) NULL (auto). Default NULL. max_starts Integer. Number restarts. Default 1. progress_mode Character: "console", "shiny", "". Default "console".

title: Optimize a vector or matrix to match target means and SDs — optim_vec
text: stats2data.object list components best_error,   data, inputs, track_error, error_msgs.

title: Optimize a vector or matrix to match target means and SDs — optim_vec
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- stats2data::optim_vec(   N           = 100,   target_mean = c(x = 10.23),   target_sd   = c(x = 2.11),   range       = c(0, 20),   integer     = TRUE,   sprite_prec = c(2, 2),   max_iter    = 50000,   max_starts  = 2 ) } # }

what: Run an optimization function multiple times in parallel — parallel_optim
title: Run an optimization function multiple times in parallel — parallel_optim
text: Wrapper executes optim_* function repeatedly collects results.

title: Run an optimization function multiple times in parallel — parallel_optim
code: parallel_optim(   FUN,   args,   runs = 10L,   cores = 1L,   seed = NULL,   progress_mode = "console" )

title: Run an optimization function multiple times in parallel — parallel_optim
text: FUN Function. One optim_vec, optim_mlr, optim_aov. args List. Named arguments forwarded FUN. progress_mode silently forced "" inside workers. runs Integer. Number independent replications. Default 10. cores Integer. Number parallel workers. Default 1 (sequential). Values > 1 require future future.apply packages. seed Integer NULL. non-NULL, passed future.apply::future_lapply future.seed reproducibility. Default NULL. progress_mode Character: "console", "shiny", "". Controls outer progress bar ; individual runs always execute silently. Default "console".

title: Run an optimization function multiple times in parallel — parallel_optim
text: object class stats2data_parallel — list : results List length runs. element object     returned FUN (class stats2data_vec,     stats2data_mlr, stats2data_aov). best single result lowest best_error. module Character. One "vec", "mlr",     "aov", inferred class first result. runs Integer. Number runs completed.

title: Run an optimization function multiple times in parallel — parallel_optim
text: cores > 1, multisession plan set torn automatically. pre-existing future plan restored exit.

title: Run an optimization function multiple times in parallel — parallel_optim
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Parallel ANOVA runs res <- parallel_optim(   FUN  = optim_aov,   args = list(S = 30, levels = c(2, 2), ...),   runs = 20, cores = 4 ) summary(res)          # aggregated summary get_stats(res)        # pooled stats across runs get_rmse(res)         # between-run and target RMSE res$best              # single best result } # }

what: Plot RMSE distributions for a parallel result — plot.stats2data_parallel
title: Plot RMSE distributions for a parallel result — plot.stats2data_parallel
text: Visualises -run variability deviation--target RMSE distributions box--jitter plots, faceted metric.

title: Plot RMSE distributions for a parallel result — plot.stats2data_parallel
code: # S3 method for class 'stats2data_parallel' plot(x, ...)

text: x object class stats2data_parallel. ... Currently unused.

title: Plot RMSE distributions for a parallel result — plot.stats2data_parallel

title: Plot RMSE distributions for a parallel result — plot.stats2data_parallel
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- parallel_optim(FUN = optim_aov, args = list(...), runs = 20) plot(res) } # }

what: Plot cooling schedule for a stats2data result — plot_cooling.stats2data_aov
title: Plot cooling schedule for a stats2data result — plot_cooling.stats2data_aov

code: # S3 method for class 'stats2data_aov' plot_cooling(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'stats2data_mlr' plot_cooling(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'stats2data_vec' plot_cooling(x, ...)  plot_cooling(x, ...)

title: Plot cooling schedule for a stats2data result — plot_cooling.stats2data_aov
text: x stats2data result object (stats2data_aov, stats2data_mlr, stats2data_vec). ... Arguments passed methods.

title: Plot cooling schedule for a stats2data result — plot_cooling.stats2data_aov
text: ggplot object, returned invisibly. ggplot object, returned invisibly. ggplot object, returned invisibly. ggplot object, returned invisibly.

title: Plot cooling schedule for a stats2data result — plot_cooling.stats2data_aov
text: plot_cooling(stats2data_aov): Method ANOVA results (stats2data_aov). plot_cooling(stats2data_mlr): Method MLR results (stats2data_mlr). plot_cooling(stats2data_vec): Method Descriptives results (stats2data_vec).

title: Plot cooling schedule for a stats2data result — plot_cooling.stats2data_aov
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- optim_aov(...) plot_cooling(res) } # }

what: Plot error trajectory for a stats2data result — plot_error.stats2data_aov
title: Plot error trajectory for a stats2data result — plot_error.stats2data_aov
text: Visualizes objective-function error evolves across iterations simulated-annealing optimiser.  Dispatches class-specific methods stats2data_aov, stats2data_mlr, stats2data_vec.

title: Plot error trajectory for a stats2data result — plot_error.stats2data_aov
code: # S3 method for class 'stats2data_aov' plot_error(x, run = 1L, show_best = TRUE, first_iter = 1L, ...)  # S3 method for class 'stats2data_mlr' plot_error(   x,   run = 1L,   show_best = TRUE,   first_iter = 1L,   ratio = FALSE,   show_mean = TRUE,   show_median = TRUE,   show_final = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'stats2data_vec' plot_error(x, run = 1L, show_best = TRUE, first_iter = 1L, ...)  plot_error(x, ...)

title: Plot error trajectory for a stats2data result — plot_error.stats2data_aov
text: x stats2data result object (stats2data_aov, stats2data_mlr, stats2data_vec). run Integer. Index run (variable) plot; default 1. show_best Logical. TRUE (default), marks minimum error. first_iter Integer. Iterations skip; default 1. ... Arguments passed methods (see individual method pages). ratio Logical. TRUE, plot correlation--regression error ratio instead total error trajectory. Default FALSE. show_mean Logical. ratio = TRUE, draw horizontal line mean ratio. Default TRUE. Ignored ratio = FALSE. show_median Logical. ratio = TRUE, draw horizontal line median ratio. Default TRUE. Ignored ratio = FALSE. show_final Logical. ratio = TRUE, draw horizontal line final ratio. Default TRUE. Ignored ratio = FALSE.

title: Plot error trajectory for a stats2data result — plot_error.stats2data_aov
text: ggplot object, returned invisibly. ggplot object, returned invisibly. ggplot object, returned invisibly. ggplot object, returned invisibly.

title: Plot error trajectory for a stats2data result — plot_error.stats2data_aov
text: stats2data_mlr objects, set ratio = TRUE plot correlation--regression error ratio instead total error.

title: Plot error trajectory for a stats2data result — plot_error.stats2data_aov
text: plot_error(stats2data_aov): Method ANOVA results (stats2data_aov). plot_error(stats2data_mlr): Method MLR results (stats2data_mlr). plot_error(stats2data_vec): Method Descriptives results (stats2data_vec).

title: Plot error trajectory for a stats2data result — plot_error.stats2data_aov
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- optim_aov(...) plot_error(res) } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- optim_mlr(...) plot_error(res) plot_error(res, ratio = TRUE) plot_error(res, ratio = TRUE, show_mean = FALSE) } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- optim_vec(...) plot_error(res) } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- optim_mlr(...) plot_error(res) plot_error(res, ratio = TRUE) plot_error(res, run = 2, first_iter = 500) } # }

what: Plot Histograms for Each Variable — plot_histogram
title: Plot Histograms for Each Variable — plot_histogram
text: Given data frame matrix numeric variables, function returns named list ggplot2 histogram (bar) plots—one per column.  variable displays vertical line mean annotation mean standard deviation (just mean).

title: Plot Histograms for Each Variable — plot_histogram
code: plot_histogram(df, tol = 1e-08, SD = TRUE)

title: Plot Histograms for Each Variable — plot_histogram
text: df data.frame matrix whose columns variables plot. tol Numeric; thresh deciding whether variable integer-valued. Values within tol integer plotted counts (bars). SD Logical; TRUE, annotate plot mean standard deviation; FALSE, annotate mean .

title: Plot Histograms for Each Variable — plot_histogram
text: named list ggplot2 objects, one per column df.

what: Partial Regression Plots for a Linear Model — plot_partial_regression
title: Partial Regression Plots for a Linear Model — plot_partial_regression
text: Given fitted linear model, function computes partial‐regression plots term model.  regress predictors plots residuals term’s residuals, annotating slope (beta) residual standard deviation.

title: Partial Regression Plots for a Linear Model — plot_partial_regression
code: plot_partial_regression(model)

title: Partial Regression Plots for a Linear Model — plot_partial_regression
text: model fitted lm model object.

title: Partial Regression Plots for a Linear Model — plot_partial_regression
text: named list ggplot2 objects, one per predictor term model.

what: Plot summary of target-vs-simulated statistics — plot_summary.stats2data_parallel
title: Plot summary of target-vs-simulated statistics — plot_summary.stats2data_parallel
text: Creates lollipop chart comparing simulated target summary statistics. Dispatches class-specific methods stats2data_aov, stats2data_mlr, stats2data_vec.

title: Plot summary of target-vs-simulated statistics — plot_summary.stats2data_parallel
code: # S3 method for class 'stats2data_parallel' plot_summary(   x,   standardised = TRUE,   eps = 1e-12,   error_type = c("range", "sd"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'stats2data_mlr' plot_summary(x, standardised = TRUE, eps = 1e-12, ...)  # S3 method for class 'stats2data_aov' plot_summary(x, standardised = TRUE, eps = 1e-12, ...)  # S3 method for class 'stats2data_vec' plot_summary(x, standardised = TRUE, eps = 1e-12, ...)  plot_summary(x, ...)

title: Plot summary of target-vs-simulated statistics — plot_summary.stats2data_parallel
text: x stats2data result object (stats2data_aov, stats2data_mlr, stats2data_vec). standardised Logical; TRUE (default), differences divided target values (except targets near zero). eps Numeric; zero-threshold. Default 1e-12. error_type Character; "range" (default) draws across-run min/max error bars, "sd" draws mean 1 SD across runs. ... Arguments passed methods (typically standardised eps).

title: Plot summary of target-vs-simulated statistics — plot_summary.stats2data_parallel
text: ggplot object, returned invisibly. ggplot object, returned invisibly. ggplot object, returned invisibly. ggplot object, returned invisibly. ggplot object, returned invisibly.

title: Plot summary of target-vs-simulated statistics — plot_summary.stats2data_parallel
text: plot_summary(stats2data_parallel): Method parallel results (stats2data_parallel). Aggregates simulated parameter across-run mean (rounded target's reported precision) overlays error bar showing across-run min/max (error_type = "range", default) mean 1 SD (error_type = "sd"). Module-specific data assembly dispatched internally based x$module. plot_summary(stats2data_mlr): Method MLR results (stats2data_mlr). plot_summary(stats2data_aov): Method ANOVA results (stats2data_aov). plot_summary(stats2data_vec): Method Descriptives results (stats2data_vec).

title: Plot summary of target-vs-simulated statistics — plot_summary.stats2data_parallel
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- parallel_optim(FUN = optim_mlr, args = list(...), runs = 20) plot_summary(res) plot_summary(res, standardised = FALSE, error_type = "sd") } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- optim_mlr(...) plot_summary(res) plot_summary(res, standardised = FALSE) } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- optim_aov(...) plot_summary(res) } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- optim_vec(...) plot_summary(res) } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- optim_vec(...) plot_summary(res) plot_summary(res, standardised = FALSE) } # }

title: Run the Shiny Application — run_app
text: Run Shiny Application

title: Run the Shiny Application — run_app
code: run_app(   onStart = NULL,   options = list(),   enableBookmarking = NULL,   uiPattern = "/",   ... )

title: Run the Shiny Application — run_app
text: onStart function called app actually run. needed shinyAppObj, since shinyAppDir case, global.R file can used purpose. options Named options passed runApp call (can following: "port", "launch.browser", "host", "quiet", "display.mode" "test.mode"). can also specify width height parameters provide hint embedding environment ideal height/width app. enableBookmarking Can one "url", "server", "disable". default value, NULL, respect setting previous calls  enableBookmarking(). See enableBookmarking() information bookmarking app. uiPattern regular expression applied GET request determine whether ui used handle request. Note entire request path must match regular expression order match considered successful. ... arguments pass golem_opts. See `?golem::get_golem_options` details.

what: Reshape Data from Wide to Long Format — wide_to_long
title: Reshape Data from Wide to Long Format — wide_to_long
text: Converts data.frame matrix wide format (ID column repeated‐measure columns named <variable>_<time>, e.g. V1_1, V1_2) long format.  resulting data columns ID, ‐subject factors, time, one column per measure.

title: Reshape Data from Wide to Long Format — wide_to_long
code: wide_to_long(data)

title: Reshape Data from Wide to Long Format — wide_to_long
text: data data.frame matrix wide format.  matrix provided, coerced data.frame.  Must least two columns matching regex "<var>_<time>".

title: Reshape Data from Wide to Long Format — wide_to_long
text: data.frame long format columns ID, ‐subject   factors (present), time (integer), one column per measure.
